summary: Reviewed the Define-phase artifacts (requirements.md, discovery_notes.md, open_questions.md) against guides/backend-guide.md and guides/ui-style-guide.md. The requirements are behavior-level and impose no implementation choices that conflict with either guide; they actively reinforce the UI a11y rule (R12/R35 = 'status never by color alone'). The discovery notes correctly internalize the mandatory constraints: complex/aggregate reads routed to app/queries/ rather than the controller, HomeController#index kept thin, broadcasts from services after commit (never model callbacks), reuse of StatusHelper#status_badge and the prescribed card/badge/empty-state/type-scale conventions, and reuse of StepRuns::Sweep fleet-health constants. R33 (per-section graceful degradation) is consistent with the services-return-Result model. The one real-time tension (the UI guide lists heartbeats as Turbo-Stream-updated, while Q9's default proposes periodic refresh for the worker panel) is explicitly raised as an open question with rationale for human decision rather than silently drifting, which is the correct handling; the guide's own 'target the smallest DOM unit' rule points to the resolution during Plan. No blocking or major guide deviations found at the design/spec stage.
